ncert_truth_statements: Kirchhoff's Voltage Law (KVL): the algebraic sum of changes in potential around any closed loop in a circuit is zero, based on conservation of energy
In applying KVL, the sum of all EMFs in a closed loop equals the sum of all IR (resistive) drops in that loop
A potential drop of IR occurs across a resistor when traversed in the direction of the current
A potential rise equal to EMF occurs when traversing a battery from its negative terminal to its positive terminal
KVL reflects the fact that electric potential energy is conserved — no net gain or loss of energy in a complete loop
